title: Running AI Locally: The Ultimate 2026 Ollama Setup Guide
excerpt: Ollama has democratised local AI. This guide breaks down my workflow for running massive LLMs (like Llama 3, Qwen) completely offline on Windows. From one-click installations and environment variables (OLLAMA_MODELS), to troubleshooting port conflicts and custom model paths. A must-have for AI developers.

title: Debugging the AS7343 spectral sensor: chip ID 0x24 and the driver rabbit hole
excerpt: A deep-dive into getting the AS7343 14-channel spectral sensor to reliably communicate over I2C on a Raspberry Pi. Covers chip ID verification (0x24), driver library quirks, and the measurement pipeline I finally settled on for EcoSortNet.

title: How TRIZ Level-1 changed the way I approach hardware problems
excerpt: After getting my TRIZ certification I started applying contradiction analysis to real engineering bottlenecks — from conveyor belt jams in EcoSortNet to sensor placement trade-offs. This is a practical breakdown of the thinking shift.

title: Building TrustGuard-AI at USM Varsity Hackathon — what we shipped in 24 hours
excerpt: Our team's experience at USM Varsity Hackathon 2026. I was responsible for the core logic and system integration of VHACK_TrustGuard-AI, an open-source repository focused on AI safety and trust protection. Here's the architecture, the trade-offs, and what I'd do differently.

title: Deploying an AI agent infrastructure on Vercel: my actual workflow
excerpt: A practical walkthrough of how I set up and manage AI agent deployments on Vercel — environment variables, edge functions, rate limiting, and how I wire the frontend Vue.js dashboard into the backend API. No fluff, just the steps that work.

title: VL53L0X + LJC18A3: combining laser ranging and inductive proximity on one conveyor
excerpt: EcoSortNet needed two fundamentally different sensing modalities to work in sync. This is the wiring diagram, the timing logic in Python, and the edge cases that took me two weeks to iron out — so you don't have to.

title: Building a real-time sensor data dashboard with Vue 3 and Tailwind
excerpt: From raw sensor readings piped over WebSockets to a live data visualisation dashboard. I cover the Vue 3 Composition API patterns, the Tailwind layout, and how to handle data that updates 10 times per second without the UI melting.

title: Raspberry Pi setup for sensor fusion projects: the guide I wish I had
excerpt: Everything from imaging the SD card and enabling I2C/SPI, to setting up a Python virtual environment, installing GPIO libraries, and testing your first sensor read — all in one place, in the order that actually makes sense.
